metadata.test_status: 123 passed + 1 skipped, zero regression on related modules

step_1_algebraic_kernel_differential.step_1_gate: PASSED (|diff_eq| = 0.135 Ha > 0.05 Ha threshold; differential at R_diss is 18.7% of R_eq value)
step_1_algebraic_kernel_differential.physical_finding: Physical Na 3s is MORE attractive to the H nucleus at R_eq than the hydrogenic Z=1 placeholder by 0.135 Ha. This is because the compact physical 3s (<r>=4.5 bohr) has more density inside R_eq=3.566 bohr where the H Coulomb tail is unscreened. The diffuse hydrogenic Z=1 3s (<r>=13.5 bohr) extends past R_eq, parts on the far side of H partially cancel. Sign of the differential is opposite to naive 'compact -> less attractive' intuition.

step_2_single_point_fci.structural_finding: Multi-zeta substitution shifts h1[0,0] (Na 3s on-site) by +57 mHa at R_eq, but the 2-electron FCI eigenvalue shifts by BIT-ZERO (-1.4e-13 Ha). Reason: the FCI ground state at NaH max_n=2 lives entirely on the H side. The H 1s orbital sees the un-screened Na Z=11 at R=3.5 bohr (h1_cross_vne[5,5] = -3.13 Ha), pulling H 1s deep to ~-3.6 Ha. Na 3s (h1[0,0] ~ -0.78 Ha) is irrelevant to the GS. The h1 eigenspectrum has the lowest 5 eigenvalues entirely on H-side states; the Na 3s diagonal shift moves only the i=5 eigenvalue, which is unoccupied in the n_e=2 GS.

step_3_mini_pes.verdict: All three architectures: monotonically descending PES, NO internal minimum. Multi-zeta has bit-zero effect at every R.

comparison_to_track_3.track_3_baseline: PK + SV (W1c+screened_valence_basis+pk_cross_center): descent 0.305 Ha, no internal minimum
comparison_to_track_3.this_sprint_W1c_alone: descent 0.252 Ha (E=-163.316 at R=2.5 vs -163.064 at R=5.0), no internal minimum
comparison_to_track_3.this_sprint_bare_baseline: descent 3.34 Ha (E=-171.097 at R=2.5 vs -167.752 at R=5.0), no internal minimum
comparison_to_track_3.this_sprint_multi_zeta_alone: bit-zero effect — not a corrective mechanism in this basis

headline_structural_finding:
The M-Y bimodule diagnostic predicted right-action (Na-side wavefunction shape) dominance for the W1c-residual orthogonality wall. The FCI test reveals the prediction does not hold for the framework's NaH max_n=2 variational state: the FCI ground state lives on the H side (over-attracted by the bare Na Z=11 nucleus), so Na-side wavefunction shape substitution has bit-zero effect on the FCI energy.

The M-Y pin-state distance was computed at fixed (c_H, c_M) = (1/sqrt(2), 1/sqrt(2)) bond character — a qualitative-shape probe assuming the GS is bonding. But the actual FCI variational state at NaH max_n=2 has c_M ~ 0 (Na valence essentially unoccupied at the GS) because the un-screened cross-V_ne attraction from Na Z=11 is much larger than the Na 3s binding energy.

The diagnostic-before-engineering rule converted a 5-6 hour wiring + FCI computation into a clean structural finding: M-Y's pin-state-distance metric is not a faithful proxy for the FCI eigenvalue sensitivity when the framework's basis is too small to support the physical bonding configuration.
verdict_paper_changes: DEFER. No paper edits autonomously applied. The structural finding is reported; the synthesis sprint (beta) will integrate.
